---
title: "Putting Custom Estimates with MTGP and Riskfolio Optimization Together"
format: html
---

# MTGP Setup

Including value and growth versions of us market etfs boosts predictions and performance. 

```{python}
import os
os.environ["PYTORCH_ENABLE_MPS_FALLBACK"] = "1"
from marketmaven.configs import TickerConfig, Interval, Horizon
from marketmaven.asset_prices import build_long_panel, fetch_etf_features
from marketmaven.market_fundamentals import fetch_macro_features
import yfinance as yf
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import riskfolio as rp
from marketmaven.configs import (
    RiskfolioConfig, OptModel, RiskMeasure, Objective, MuEstimator, CovEstimator)
from marketmaven.visualization.eda import correlation_matrix
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

    
# A shortered list for testing 
etf_tickers = [
    "SPY", # total US market big cap
    "MGK", # US growth
    "VTV", # US value
    "IJR", # US small cap
    "VNQ", # REIT ETF US centric
    "VNQI", # international REIT ETF
    "VEA", # developed international equity
    "VWO", # AVEM actually is better than VWO but not enough history
    "VSS", # forein small/mid cap
    "BND", # total bond market ETF US centric
    "IEF", # 7-10 year treasury bond ETF US centric
    "BNDX", # total international bond market ETF, USD hedged, but actually developed markets only
    "LQD", # investment grade bond ETF US centric
    "HYG", # High yield bond ETF US centric 
    "TIP", # Treasury inflation protected securities ETF US centric
    "EMB", # emerging market bond ETF USD hedged
    "EWX", # emerging market small cap dividend ETF
    #"PDBC", # Commodities ETF
    #"BIL", # 1-3 month us treasuries 
    
]

# ##### Others to consider
# 	•	VTV (Value tilt) — improves factor diversification (value vs growth).
# 	•	MGK (Growth tilt) — if you want symmetrical exposure to growth.

# #commodity alternatives 
#     •	BCI — cheaper, more systematic exposure
# 	•	DBC — classic diversified commodities (higher AUM)

#     BWX — International Treasury Bonds (Unhedged)
#     If the USD weakens, BWX will:
# 	•	Increase in value due to currency appreciation
# 	•	Add macro diversification
#     XLE or IXC — Energy sector (pure play inflation hedge)
#     If you want stronger inflation beta than commodities ETFs provide.

#     GLDM or IAU — Gold ETF
#     Gold adds:
# 	•	Crisis alpha
# 	•	Negative correlation to real rates
# 	•	Deep diversification vs equities

#     VIOV or AVUV — U.S. small-cap value
#     Even small allocations improve:
# 	•	Risk-adjusted returns
# 	•	Factor diversification
# 	•	Downside risk reduction

#     DGS — WisdomTree Emerging Markets SmallCap Dividend Fund, or EWX which is more balanced
#     	•	True EM small-cap exposure
# 	•	Emphasizes quality + dividend tilt
# 	•	More diversifying than VWO or SCHE

#Correlation with SPY ≈ 0.0–0.1

config = TickerConfig(
    start_date="2013-06-01",
    end_date="2025-11-02",
    interval=Interval.DAILY,
    tickers=etf_tickers,
    horizon=Horizon.MONTHLY,
)

return_data = build_long_panel(config.tickers, config.start_date, config.end_date, horizon=config.horizon)


pivoted_returns = return_data.pivot(index="date", columns="asset_id", values="y_excess_lead")

pivoted_returns = pivoted_returns.dropna().sort_index()

display(pivoted_returns.head())

y = (
    pivoted_returns
)

# Optional: remove column index name and return to a regular column layout
y.columns.name = None
y = y.reset_index()
y.index = y['date']
y = y.drop(columns=['date'])

y.head(10)

fig = correlation_matrix(pivoted_returns)

fig.show()

```

## Factors 

```{python}
from marketmaven.utils import check_equal_occurrences
#Market Level Features
macro_features = fetch_macro_features(start=config.start_date, end=config.end_date)
macro_features.hist()

#ETF price features

etf_features = fetch_etf_features(config.tickers, config.start_date, config.end_date, config.horizon)
etf_features


#combine all factor columns
df = (
    return_data
    .merge(etf_features, on=['asset_id', 'date'], how='left')
    .merge(macro_features, on='date', how='left')
)

# drop some columns like "volume" which is redundant
df = df.drop(columns=[ 'vix', 'log_ret', 'dxy', 'yc_pc1', 
       'yc_pc2', 'yc_pc3'])

all_assets_occur = check_equal_occurrences(df, 'asset_id')
print('Did all assets occur equally often?', all_assets_occur)

df["t_index"] = df.groupby("asset_id").cumcount()

feature_cols = ["t_index", 'price', 'mom1m',
       'mom6m', 'mom12m', 'mom36m', 'chmom', 'volume', 'dolvol', 'turnover',
       'sd_turn', 'ill', 'vol_1m', 'baspread', 'vix3m', 'vix_ts_level',
       'vix_ts_chg_1m', 'vix_ts_z_12m', 'tnote10y','tbill3m', 'term_spread',
       'credit_spread', 'credit_spread_chg_1p', ]

# Move column t_index to the first column position 
cols = df.columns.tolist()       
cols.insert(0, cols.pop(cols.index("t_index")))

df = df[cols]

fig = correlation_matrix(df)
fig.show()
```

## Data transformation for MTGP

```{python}

from marketmaven.gp_data_prep import prepare_multitask_gp_data
from marketmaven.models.cv import rolling_time_splits_multitask
from marketmaven.models.scaling import MultitaskScaler
device = torch.device("cpu")

X, I, y, task_map = prepare_multitask_gp_data(
    df,
    target_col="y_excess_lead",
    asset_col="asset_id",
    drop_cols=["date", "asset_id"]
)
window_size = 1
index = -1 * len(task_map) * window_size
eps = 1e-8
X_tr, I_tr, y_tr = X[:index], I[:index], y[:index]
X_te, I_te, y_te = X[index:], I[index:], y[index:]

# Initialize and fit
scaler = MultitaskScaler(scale_y="global", exclude_time_col=False)
scaler.fit_x(X_tr)
X_trs = scaler.transform_x(X_tr)
X_tes = scaler.transform_x(X_te)

y_trs = scaler.fit_y(y_tr, I_tr)

# concat the I_tr and I_te to the X_trs and X_tes respectively
X_trs = torch.cat([X_trs, I_tr.to(X_trs.dtype)], dim=-1)
X_tes = torch.cat([X_tes, I_te.to(X_tes.dtype)], dim=-1)

X_trs = X_trs.to(device)
y_trs = y_trs.to(device)
X_tes = X_tes.to(device)

```



## Setup Hadamard GP

```{python}

from marketmaven.models.gp import train_model_hadamard
from gpytorch.means import ConstantMean, ZeroMean, LinearMean
from gpytorch.kernels import RBFKernel, ScaleKernel, PeriodicKernel, LinearKernel, MaternKernel, SpectralMixtureKernel, RQKernel
from math import sqrt, log
from gpytorch.priors import LogNormalPrior
from gpytorch.constraints import GreaterThan
import torch
from gpytorch.means import MultitaskMean, ConstantMean, Mean

import torch
from gpytorch.means import Mean


from marketmaven.models.means import compute_ewma2_means_torch, EWMATaskConstantMean

SQRT2 = sqrt(2)
SQRT3 = sqrt(3)
# set seed torch for reproducibility
torch.manual_seed(42)

ard_num_dims = len(feature_cols) -1
active_dims_features = list(range(1, ard_num_dims + 1))
lengthscale_prior = LogNormalPrior(loc=SQRT2 + log(ard_num_dims) * 0.5, scale=SQRT3)
lengthscale_constraint = GreaterThan(2.5e-2, initial_value=lengthscale_prior.mode)
lengthscale_prior_time = LogNormalPrior(loc=SQRT2 + log(1) * 0.3, scale=SQRT3)
lengthscale_constraint_time = GreaterThan(2.5e-2, initial_value=lengthscale_prior_time.mode)

def adaptive_lengthscale_prior(num_dims:int):
    return LogNormalPrior(loc=SQRT2 + log(num_dims) * 0.5, scale=SQRT3)
def adaptive_lengthscale_constraint(num_dims:int):
    return GreaterThan(2.5e-2, initial_value=adaptive_lengthscale_prior(num_dims).mode)


num_tasks = len(torch.unique(I_tr))

mean_f = MultitaskMean(
    ConstantMean(),
    num_tasks=num_tasks,
)
kernel_f = MaternKernel(
    nu=0.5,
    ard_num_dims=ard_num_dims,
    lengthscale_prior=adaptive_lengthscale_prior(ard_num_dims),
    lengthscale_constraint=adaptive_lengthscale_constraint(ard_num_dims),
    active_dims=active_dims_features,
) 


n_months = len(np.unique(X_tr.numpy()[:,0]))
period_length = 12.0
period_length = 12.0 / n_months

k_per = PeriodicKernel(active_dims=[0], period_length=period_length,             period_length_prior=LogNormalPrior(loc=0.0, scale=0.3),
    lengthscale_prior=adaptive_lengthscale_prior(1),
    lengthscale_constraint=adaptive_lengthscale_constraint(1),)

kernel_t = MaternKernel(
    nu=2.5,
    ard_num_dims=1,
    lengthscale_prior=adaptive_lengthscale_prior(1),
    lengthscale_constraint=adaptive_lengthscale_constraint(1),
    active_dims=[0],
) + k_per 


kernel_total = kernel_f * kernel_t


model, likelihood = train_model_hadamard(X_trs, y_trs, rank=6, mean_f=mean_f, kernel=kernel_total,training_iterations=500, patience=50, visualize=True, task_feature=-1, dtype=torch.float32, device=torch.device("cpu"))



```


## Evaluation 

```{python}
import torch
import gpytorch
from marketmaven.models.metrics import gaussian_nlpd, mse, rmse, r2_score



model.eval(); likelihood.eval()
with torch.no_grad():
    f_dist = model(X_tes)
    pred = likelihood(f_dist, X_tes)  # pass X_te so per-task noise is applied


yhat = scaler.inverse_y(pred.mean, I_te)
ystd = scaler.inverse_std(pred.variance.sqrt(), I_te)


# Per-task
# Build inverse mapping: index -> ETF ticker
inv_task_map = {v: k for k, v in task_map.items()}

task_vals = I_te.view(-1).to(torch.long)
task_values = {}

for k in torch.unique(task_vals):
    k_mask = (task_vals == k)

    etf_name = inv_task_map[int(k.item())]  # <-- get the ETF name

    y_true_k = y_te[k_mask]
    y_mean_k = yhat[k_mask]
    y_std_k  = ystd[k_mask]

    nlpd_k = gaussian_nlpd(y_true_k, y_mean_k, y_std_k)
    mse_k  = mse(y_true_k, y_mean_k)
    rmse_k = rmse(y_true_k, y_mean_k)
    r2_k   = r2_score(y_true_k, y_mean_k)

    task_values[etf_name] = {
        'NLPD': nlpd_k,
        'MSE': mse_k,
        'RMSE': rmse_k,
        'R2': r2_k,
    }

    print(f"{etf_name} (Task {int(k.item())}): "
          f"NLPD={nlpd_k:.3f}, MSE={mse_k:.3f}, RMSE={rmse_k:.3f}, R2={r2_k:.3f}")

#### additional metrics 
from marketmaven.evaluate import evaluate_asset_pricing

y_pred = yhat.numpy()
y_pred = y_pred.reshape(window_size, len(task_map))
y_pred = pd.DataFrame(y_pred, columns=task_map.values())


y_true = y_te.numpy()
y_true = y_true.reshape(window_size, len(task_map))
y_true = pd.DataFrame(y_true, columns=task_map.values())

# mean historical excess return as benchmark
y_train_mean = y_tr.numpy().reshape(-1, len(task_map)).mean(0)
y_train_mean= pd.DataFrame(
    np.tile(y_train_mean, (window_size, 1)),
    columns=task_map.values()
)

# ewma2
from riskfolio.src.ParamsEstimation import mean_vector
historical = y_tr.numpy().reshape(-1, len(task_map))
historical = pd.DataFrame(historical, columns=task_map.values())
ewm = mean_vector(X=historical, method="ewma2")
ewm = pd.DataFrame(
    np.tile(ewm.values, (window_size, 1)),
    columns=task_map.values()
)


results_pred = evaluate_asset_pricing(y_true, y_pred)
results_mean = evaluate_asset_pricing(y_true, y_train_mean)
results_ewm = evaluate_asset_pricing(y_true, ewm)

print(f"Results for MTGP predictions: {results_pred}")
print(f"Results for historical mean benchmark: {results_mean}")
print(f"Results for EWMA benchmark: {results_ewm}")

```

# Riskfolio Setup 


```{python}


pd.options.display.float_format = '{:.4%}'.format
display(pivoted_returns.tail())

```


## Estimating Cvar model 

```{python}
import riskfolio as rp
from marketmaven.configs import (
    RiskfolioConfig, OptModel, RiskMeasure, Objective, MuEstimator, CovEstimator)

risk_config = RiskfolioConfig(
    model=OptModel.CLASSIC,
    rm=RiskMeasure.CVaR,
    obj=Objective.SHARPE,
    method_mu=MuEstimator.EWMA2,
    method_cov=CovEstimator.GERBER2,

)

# Building the portfolio object
port = rp.Portfolio(returns=pivoted_returns)

# Calculating optimal portfolio

# Select method and estimate input parameters:

method_mu='hist' # Method to estimate expected returns based on historical data.
method_cov='hist' # Method to estimate covariance matrix based on historical data.

port.assets_stats(method_mu=risk_config.method_mu, method_cov=risk_config.method_cov)

# Estimate optimal portfolio:

model_risk=risk_config.model # Could be Classic (historical), BL (Black Litterman) or FM (Factor Model)
rm = risk_config.rm # Risk measure used, this time will be variance
obj = risk_config.obj# Objective function, could be MinRisk, MaxRet, Utility or Sharpe
hist = True # Use historical scenarios for risk measures that depend on scenarios
rf = 0 # Risk free rate
l = 0 # Risk aversion factor, only useful when obj is 'Utility'

port.card = None 

# Then we need to set the constraint on the minimum number of effective assets
port.nea = 12

w = port.optimization(model=model_risk, rm=rm, obj=obj, rf=rf, l=l, hist=hist)

display(w.T)
label = str(risk_config.rm) + " " + str(risk_config.obj) + " Adjusted Return Portfolio" # Title of point
# Plotting the composition of the portfolio

ax = rp.plot_pie(w=w, title=label, others=0.05, nrow=25, cmap = "tab20",
                 height=6, width=10, ax=None)

```

## Efficient Frontier

```{python}
points = 50 # Number of points of the frontier
pd.options.display.float_format = '{:.4%}'.format
frontier = port.efficient_frontier(model=model_risk, rm=rm, points=points, rf=rf, hist=hist)

display(frontier.T.head())




mu = port.mu # Expected returns
cov = port.cov # Covariance matrix
returns = port.returns # Returns of the assets

ax = rp.plot_frontier(w_frontier=frontier, mu=mu, cov=cov, returns=returns, rm=rm,
                      rf=rf, alpha=0.05, cmap='viridis', w=w, label=label,
                      marker='*', s=16, c='r', height=6, width=10, ax=None)


```


```{python}
# Plotting efficient frontier composition

ax = rp.plot_frontier_area(w_frontier=frontier, cmap="tab20", height=6, width=10, ax=None)

```

## Predict 

```{python}

y_pred = yhat.numpy()
y_pred = y_pred.reshape(window_size, len(task_map))
y_pred = pd.DataFrame(y_pred, columns=task_map.keys())

y_std = ystd.numpy()
y_std = y_std.reshape(window_size, len(task_map))
y_std = pd.DataFrame(y_std, columns=task_map.keys())


y_true = y_te.numpy()
y_true = y_true.reshape(window_size, len(task_map))
y_true = pd.DataFrame(y_true, columns=task_map.keys())


B = model.task_covar_module.covar_matrix.detach().cpu()
print(B.shape)  # should be [num_tasks, num_tasks]
diag = B.diag().sqrt().clamp_min(1e-12)
corr = B / (diag.unsqueeze(1) * diag.unsqueeze(0))
inv_task_map = {v: k for k, v in task_map.items()}
etf_labels = [inv_task_map[i] for i in range(len(inv_task_map))]
task_covar_df = pd.DataFrame(
    corr.numpy(),
    index=etf_labels,
    columns=etf_labels
)

import plotly.express as px

fig = px.imshow(
    task_covar_df,
    text_auto=True,          # shows the correlation values
    color_continuous_scale='RdBu',
    zmin=-1, zmax=1,         # fix scale for correlation
    aspect='auto',
)

fig.update_layout(
    title="MTGP Learned Task Correlation Matrix",
    xaxis_title="ETF",
    yaxis_title="ETF",
    width=900,
    height=900,
)

fig.show()


# --- Convert MTGP correlation to numpy ---
corr_np = corr.detach().cpu().numpy()

# --- Extract std per asset ---
std_vec = y_std.iloc[0].values.astype(float)

# --- Build covariance ---
cov_mat = corr_np * np.outer(std_vec, std_vec)

cov_df = pd.DataFrame(
    cov_mat,
    index=etf_labels,
    columns=etf_labels
)
```

```{python}

port = rp.Portfolio(returns=pivoted_returns)
risk_config = RiskfolioConfig(
    model=OptModel.CLASSIC,
    rm=RiskMeasure.MV,
    obj=Objective.SHARPE,
    method_mu=MuEstimator.EWMA2,
    method_cov=CovEstimator.GERBER2,

)

model_risk=risk_config.model 
rm = risk_config.rm 
obj = risk_config.obj
hist = True 
rf = 0 # Risk free rate
l = 0 # Risk aversion factor, only useful when obj is 'Utility'

# your predicted 1-step-ahead returns from MTGP
mu = y_pred.iloc[0]                     # Series of length n_assets
mu.index = etf_labels                   # align labels
mu = mu.astype(float)
mu_df = mu.to_frame()                # Riskfolio expects a row vector
port.mu = mu_df.T         # Make it a column vector
port.cov = cov_df         # Use your predictive covariance

port.nea = 14

w = port.optimization(model=model_risk, rm=rm, obj=obj, rf=rf, l=l, hist=hist)

display(w.T)
label = str(risk_config.rm) + " " + str(risk_config.obj) + " Adjusted Return Portfolio" # Title of point
# Plotting the composition of the portfolio

ax = rp.plot_pie(w=w, title=label, others=0.05, nrow=25, cmap = "tab20",
                 height=6, width=10, ax=None)

```

```{python}
points = 50 # Number of points of the frontier
pd.options.display.float_format = '{:.4%}'.format
frontier = port.efficient_frontier(model=model_risk, rm=rm, points=points, rf=rf, hist=hist)

display(frontier.T.head())




mu = port.mu # Expected returns
cov = port.cov # Covariance matrix
returns = port.returns # Returns of the assets

ax = rp.plot_frontier(w_frontier=frontier, mu=mu, cov=cov, returns=returns, rm=rm,
                      rf=rf, alpha=0.05, cmap='viridis', w=w, label=label,
                      marker='*', s=16, c='r', height=6, width=10, ax=None)


```
